properties.titulo_evento: Neymar quits international football after Brazil’s World Cup knockout loss
properties.descricao: The injury-plagued striker converted a penalty but failed to take his team into the last 16 of the FIFA World Cup.

properties.titulo_evento: Belgium ‘astonished’ at FIFA’s U-turn on Balogun red card for USA match
properties.descricao: Belgium's football federation is 'investigating all potential options' to safeguard football after FIFA's U-turn.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran war live: Tehran prepares for Ali Khamenei’s funeral procession
properties.descricao: Millions expected at funeral procession for Iran's slain supreme leader in Tehran today.

properties.descricao: After the June 24 earthquakes, ex-firefighter Jesus Garcia found himself working to save his dad and younger brothers.

properties.descricao: Haaland's brace takes him to seven goals for the tournament as Norway book a quarterfinal against Mexico or England.

properties.descricao: Israeli forces also shoot dead a 16-year-old boy in the occupied West Bank and kill two Palestinians in Gaza.

properties.titulo_evento: Volunteers vow to keep searching for Venezuela earthquake survivors
properties.descricao: Rescue teams in Venezuela’s La Guaira State say they’re never going to give up their search for earthquake survivors.

properties.descricao: Balogun was set to miss USA vs Belgium last 16 tie but will now be available as FIFA surprisingly ends his suspension.

properties.descricao: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Sunday that some Christian villages in southern Lebanon had asked to be annexed by Israel for protection from Hezbollah, a claim made as he reaffirmed that Israeli troops would remain in southern Leb...

properties.titulo_evento: Thieves steal around €4 million in jewellery from French museum
properties.descricao: Jewellery worth several million euros has been stolen from the Lalique Museum in northeastern France after burglars broke into the site early on Sunday and targeted its jewellery collection. Around 20 pieces were taken in the raid, with losses estim...

properties.titulo_evento: Marine Le Pen faces key appeal ruling over 2027 presidential bid
properties.descricao: France's far-right chief Marine Le Pen faces a key ruling next week that will determine if she can run in the 2027 presidential elections, in what is seen as her best chance at the top job. A lower court handed the 57-year-old politician a five-year...

properties.titulo_evento: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei absent from mass funeral for father
properties.descricao: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s son and successor was not among the family members and thousands of mourners who turned out Sunday for a second day of ceremonies to honour Iran’s late supreme leader. Mojtaba Khamenei has remained out of the public eye sinc...

properties.titulo_evento: Tour de France stage to go ahead with changes as fires ravage southern Europe
properties.descricao: An "adapted" format of the third stage of the Tour de France will go ahead on Monday after wildfires sweeping southern France put the cycling stage in jeopardy, a top official said Sunday. The blaze, which has already scorched 1,500 hectares near th...

properties.titulo_evento: Iran needs to 'normalise relations with the US somehow' to save economy, expert says

properties.titulo_evento: Venezuela quakes final death toll expected to be 'much higher'
properties.descricao: Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes killed nearly 3,000, updated official figures showed on Saturday, as international rescue teams began winding down search operations for survivors in the rubble of the disaster. FRANCE 24's Maxime Pluvinet re...

properties.titulo_evento: Paris appeals court ruling could upend Marine Le Pen’s 2027 presidential bid
properties.descricao: Paris' appeals court is set to rule Tuesday in Marine Le Pen’s embezzlement case, a decision that could determine whether one of France’s leading presidential contenders can run in next year’s election.

properties.titulo_evento: The week in pictures: America at 250, Taylor Swift gets married and a 'massive attack' on Kyiv
properties.descricao: America marks 250 years of independence under a heatwave, a massive Russian attack sets Kyiv ablaze, France dominates Sweden at the World Cup, and Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce get married at Madison Square Garden: FRANCE 24 looks back at some of th...

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine: Renewed attacks on Kyiv leave several killed
properties.descricao: The Ukrainian capital was hit by Russian ballistic missiles, with at least one building being hit. This comes after the large-scale attack on Kyiv last Thursday.

properties.titulo_evento: Wildfires rage in Portugal, Greece, France and Spain
properties.descricao: Six countries in southern Europe have been hit by wildfires. One of the blazes, in southwestern France, could affect Monday's third stage of the Tour de France, authorities warn.

properties.titulo_evento: Students leave Gaza to continue studies, others in limbo
properties.descricao: More than three school years since the start of Israel's war in Gaza, more Palestinian students are restarting studies at universities abroad. Education has yet to return to normal, as schools expand online teaching.

properties.titulo_evento: Cargo ship reportedly attacked in Red Sea off Yemen
properties.descricao: The UK Maritime Trade Office said a vessel was attacked 30 nautical miles southwest of Hodeida, which is under control of the Houthi rebels. The Iran-backed group recently vowed to resume attacks in the Red Sea.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran: Pressure on persecuted Baha'i community intensifies
properties.descricao: Arrests, torture, mock executions: War has seen the Iranian regime crack down on dissidents of all kinds. Members of the country's Baha'i faith have long been persecuted, but their situation is now worsening.

properties.titulo_evento: Trump set to put NATO unity to the test at Turkey summit
properties.descricao: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte is working to keep the alliance united and Donald Trump engaged. But disputes over burden sharing, US military commitments, and the Iran war are creating a major test for NATO unity.

properties.titulo_evento: Jehovah's Witnesses fight German state for Holocaust archive
properties.descricao: The Jehovah's Witnesses have secured a partial victory in a dispute over a unique archive documenting the community's persecution by the Nazi regime. It comes after a new memorial to the victims was unveiled in Berlin.

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine strikes oil terminals near St. Petersburg in Russia
properties.descricao: The long-range strikes are the latest salvo in Ukraine’s expanding campaign to inflict economic damage on Russia and hinder its war machine.

properties.titulo_evento: Kielce pogrom in Poland: 80th anniversary of 1946 massacre
properties.descricao: Just over a year after World War II ended, a mob in the Polish city of Kielce killed around 40 Jews, some of them Holocaust survivors. A lie about ritual child murder escalated the violence.

properties.titulo_evento: France’s far-right star Bardella is about to find out whether he’s running for president
properties.descricao: The 30-year-old National Rally president will become his party's candidate if Marine Le Pen's appeal fails on Tuesday.

properties.titulo_evento: ‘Shame on you’: Belgium accuses FIFA of caving to Trump ahead of World Cup clash
properties.descricao: Politicians across the political spectrum blasted the decision to clear American star Folarin Balogun to play Monday.

properties.titulo_evento: German defense chief opposes sharing intel with AfD ministers
properties.descricao: Defense Minister Boris Pistorius cited the far-right party's close links with Moscow and Russian President Vladimir Putin as the main reason.

properties.titulo_evento: OPEC Plus Pledges to Pump More Even as Oil Prices Fall
properties.descricao: The cartel’s decision comes as U.S.-Iran talks to end the war have eased pressure on oil supplies, even as broader market and political volatility persists.
